summary: PR 2740 was a data-matching change for `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax`. It updated symbol metadata, moved or materialized several `lbaudio_ax` data objects into `lbaudio_ax.static.h`, adjusted static storage declarations, and added explicit `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` order-hack functions. The bot report showed large data gains and complete matches for `.bss`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sbss`, but also one broken code match and several unmatched-function score regressions, many corresponding to bodies wrapped in `#if 0` in the diff.

key_files.role: Updated GALE01 symbol metadata for `lbaudio_ax` data objects: scope annotations, corrected object sizes, and split string/table symbols around addresses such as `803BBCFC` and `803BCA80`.

Removed some local data definitions and padding, moved function-pointer table placement, added `.data`/`.sdata`/`.sdata2` order-hack functions, and wrapped multiple recovered code bodies in `#if 0` comments.

Materialized or relocated static data definitions for `lbaudio_ax`, including arrays of flags, SSM/HPS strings, offset tables, static globals, and reduced `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc` to its leading matched portion with separate static arrays for following BSS regions.

what_changed.text: Updated symbol metadata for `lbaudio_ax` data objects, including adding `scope:local`, changing sizes such as `s32_arr_803BB6B0` from `0x150` to `0x14D`, splitting `str_last_ssm` and `lbl_803BBCFC`, and splitting `lbl_803BCA4C`/`lbl_803BCAC8` into several local string symbols.

Changed several declarations in `lbaudio_ax.static.h` from `extern` to `static` and added static definitions for BSS/SBSS-like data such as `lbl_804D63F0` through `lbl_804D6404`, `lbl_804D3870`, and `lbl_804D3878`.

Reshaped `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc` to include only fields through offset `0xB4` and added a `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc) == 0xB4)`, while representing following storage as separate static arrays at address-commented offsets.

Moved or introduced initialized data arrays in the static header, including `flags_arr_803BB800`, `lbl_803BBCFC` SSM string table, `lbl_803BC314` HPS string table, and `offsets_arr_803BC4E4` initialized contents.
Removed padding globals such as `data_pad_0` and `data_pad` from `lbaudio_ax.c` and moved `lbl_803BC314` out of the C file into the static header.
Added explicit order-hack helper functions: `order_data`, `order_sdata`, and `order_sdata2`, containing `(void)` references to strings, `__FILE__`, and `1.88f` to influence data ordering.
Wrapped multiple code bodies in `#if 0` while leaving marker comments such as `/// #lbAudioAx_80023B24`, `/// #fn_800267B0`, `/// #lbAudioAx_8002838C`, and `/// #lbAudioAx_80028690`.

The automated report recorded new complete matches for `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` `.bss`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sbss`, with matched data increasing by 134816 bytes, while matched code decreased by 260 bytes.

smart_moves.text: The PR used symbol metadata corrections and source data materialization together, aligning `symbols.txt` object boundaries with static declarations in `lbaudio_ax.static.h`.
Address comments on separated BSS arrays, such as `/* 4337C4 */ static int lbl_804337C4[0x38];`, preserved layout intent after shrinking the visible `lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc` struct.
The author labeled order-forcing helpers with `@todo` comments, making the hacks visible as cleanup candidates rather than presenting them as ordinary authored style.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For data-focused work, symbol-boundary corrections in `symbols.txt` may need to be paired with corresponding source declarations and initialized tables; the useful record is the address, size, scope, and data-kind change, not just the C initializer.

When a broad struct was previously used to cover adjacent storage, one matching tactic is to shrink it to the proven leading fields and represent later address ranges as separate static arrays with address comments and size assertions.
Data-match PRs can trade off code matching: this PR gained complete `lbaudio_ax` data-section matches while the bot reported a broken matched code function and several unmatched-function regressions.

naming_conventions.text: The PR retained address-style names for many data objects, such as `lbl_803BBCFC`, `lbl_803BC314`, `lbl_803BCA80`, and `lbl_804337C4`, instead of inventing semantic names for recovered data tables.
Some known asset-string symbols kept descriptive `str_*` names, such as `str_last_ssm`, `str_1p_qk_hps`, and `str_lbAudioLoadData`, matching existing symbol-file style for string-literal data.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Correct data symbol sizes, scopes, and splits in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to align object boundaries with actual data layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Requires data ownership and boundary evidence; the PR evidence shows the edits and bot data gains but not the underlying manual verification process.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Materialize previously extern or padded data as static definitions in `lbaudio_ax.static.h`, including initialized string tables and numeric tables.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Large static data moves can perturb code and nearby data ordering; bot output showed data gains and code regressions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit `order_data`, `order_sdata`, and `order_sdata2` helper functions with `(void)` references to force string/float ordering.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This is a data-order hack and conflicts with modern standards that discourage fake data anchors or literal promotion unless narrowly justified.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Temporarily exclude code bodies with `#if 0` marker comments to allow data-section matching to proceed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The bot reported code regressions, including `fn_800267B0` dropping from 100% to 0%, so this should be treated as historical data-match triage rather than a general code-decomp tactic.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Automated GALE01 report showed matched data improved to 59.61% (+11.13%, +134816 bytes), with four new complete matches for `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` sections: `.bss`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sbss`.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: The same report showed matched code decreased by 260 bytes and listed `fn_800267B0` as a broken match, plus regressions for functions including `lbAudioAx_80028690`, `lbAudioAx_80027AB0`, `lbAudioAx_8002838C`, `lbAudioAx_80023B24`, `lbAudioAx_80027168`, `fn_800269AC`, `fn_80026C04`, and `lbAudioAx_80027648`.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice, and the PR body was empty.

notes: Strong evidence for changed files, symbol edits, visible source edits, merge metadata, and automated match report.
The raw diff and PR JSON are marked truncated, so not every initializer or hunk is visible in the loaded slice.
The PR body was empty and no human review comments were loaded, so author intent and reviewer rationale cannot be inferred beyond the title, diff, and bot report.
The automated report proves match-score changes for that PR range, but the slice does not include build logs or objdiff details for each specific edit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2740: `lbaudio_ax` data matching used symbol-boundary corrections plus static data materialization
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2740, data matching for `main/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax` combined `symbols.txt` boundary/scope changes with moving or materializing data tables in `src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.static.h`. The bot reported complete matches for the unit's `.bss`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `.sbss`, but also code-match regressions.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2740: data-section matching may expose code-match tradeoffs
accepted_candidate_records.text: The automated report for PR 2740 shows a large `lbaudio_ax` data improvement alongside matched-code loss and several function regressions. Treat this as evidence that data-order changes and excluding code bodies can improve data metrics while harming code metrics, not as a general endorsement of the tactic.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify handling of explicit data-order helper functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2740 contains `order_data`, `order_sdata`, and `order_sdata2` helpers with `(void)` references to strings, `__FILE__`, and a float literal, labeled as order hacks. Existing standards already discourage fake data anchors and require evidence for matching tactics; curator may consider adding this as an example of a historical exception pattern that should be narrowly justified and cleaned up when possible.
source_update_candidates.reason: The diff provides a concrete historical example, but intake should not promote it as accepted style because current standards treat fake anchors and broad matching tactics as risky.

source_update_candidates.title: `lbaudio_ax.static.h` has historically owned large local data tables for `lbaudio_ax` matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2740 moved or materialized multiple `lbaudio_ax` data objects into `src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.static.h`, including SSM/HPS string tables, flags, offset tables, and static globals, while `symbols.txt` was adjusted to match local object boundaries.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped historical context for future work in this translation unit; should be curated against current source before adoption.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that `#if 0`-ing code bodies is an accepted general tactic; in this PR it coincided with reported code regressions and appears tied to a data-focused change.
Do not promote the order-hack functions as accepted style; current standards require evidence for such tactics and discourage fake anchors/literal-order workarounds.
Do not infer semantic meanings for address-style symbols such as `lbl_803BBCFC` or `lbl_803BCA80`; the PR mostly preserves address-style names.
